perturbation.original_text: In this Agreement, the followi ng terms will have the following meanings: “Affiliate ” means any legal entity that a Receiving Party Controls, is Controlled by, or with which it is under common Control, where Control means to own or control, directly or indirectly, over 50% of voting shares.
perturbation.changed_text: In this Agreement, the followi ng terms will have the following meanings: “Affiliate ” means any legal entity.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the definition of 'Control' from the definition of 'Affiliate' in Section 1 creates an ambiguity. The agreement still uses the term 'Affiliate', implying that 'Control' is relevant to its definition. This contradicts the intent of the original definition where 'Control' was precisely defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving P arty may disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to those of its officers, directors, employees, (including those of its Affiliates) or Corporate Advisors who have a need to know such Confidential Information provided that such individuals are bound by obligations of confidentiality to the Receiving Party or have entered into agreements with the Receiving Party with obligations of confidentiality no less stringent than those of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Affiliate' including a definition of 'Control'. The changed text removes the definition of 'Control', making the term 'Affiliate' ambiguous in the contradicted section. The contradicted text relies on a clear understanding of who the 'Affiliates' are. Because the change introduces ambiguity by omitting the definition of Control as it applies to 'Affiliates', and it changes a core element of the original meaning of a Key Term, as it is used in the Contradicted Text, this creates a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information ” means any information which is confidential in nature or that is treated as confidential by a Party and that is furnished or transferred by or on behalf of such Party (the “ Disclosing Party ”) to the other Party (the “ Receiving Party ”), whether such information is or has been conveyed verbally or in written or tangible form, and whether such information is acquired d irectly or indirectly such as in the course of discussions, site visits or other investigations by the Receiving Party, including but not limited to, scientific, technical, financial or business information, data, ideas, concepts or know -how, formula, anal ysis, process, design, sketch, photograph, plan, drawing, specification, sample, rep ort, study, finding, prototype and non -published patent application s, that are considered and treated as being confidential by the Disclosing Party. Confidential Informatio n disclosed in tangible or electronic form may be identified by Disclosing Party as confidential with conspicuous markings, or otherwise identified with a legend as being confidential . Where the information is disclosed verbally or visually and the Disclos ing Party requires that the Receiving Party maintain the confidential nature of the information, the Disclosing Party shall express a verbal statement of confidentiality at the time of disclosure followed by confirmation 2 in writing within thirty (30) days of such disclosure . Notwithstanding the foregoing, i n no event s hall the absence of such a mark, legend or verbal confirmation preclude disclosed information , or information accessed through discussions or site visits, which would be considered as confiden tial by a party exercising reasonable judgment from being treated as Confidential Information by the Receiving Party .
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information ” means any information which is confidential in nature or that is treated as confidential by a Party and that is furnished or transferred by or on behalf of such Party (the “ Disclosing Party ”) to the other Party (the “ Receiving Party ”).
perturbation.explanation: By removing the detailed description of what constitutes 'Confidential Information' in Section 1, the definition becomes vague. The agreement continues to use this term in subsequent sections, such as Section 2, which describes how to handle said Confidential Information. By making it so vague, it creates an in-text contradiction, where the handling and usage of Confidential Information becomes uncertain and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party agrees that it shall not: 2.1.1 use any of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information for any reason other than the Purpose; or 2.1.2 disclose, disseminate or otherwise communicate, in whole or in part, any of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information to any third party.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a detailed definition of 'Confidential Information,' including examples and methods for its identification. The changed text removes this detailed description, making the definition vague. This vagueness directly contradicts Section 2, which outlines how to handle and use 'Confidential Information.' Without a clear definition, the obligations in Section 2 become uncertain and potentially unenforceable, creating a strong in-text contradiction as the 'Receiving Party' cannot clearly determine what information is subject to the restrictions outlined in 2.1.1 and 2.1.2.

perturbation.original_text: “Retained Information ” shall mean one (1) copy of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information that is retained by the Receiving Party : (i) if and to the extent such Confidential Information is required by law, regulation, administrative or court order , and (ii) where such Confidential Information is stored as electronic data due to automatic archiving and back up procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: “Retained Information ” shall mean one (1) copy of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information that is retained by the Receiving Party .
perturbation.explanation: Removing the qualifying conditions for 'Retained Information' (legal requirement or electronic storage) from Section 1 introduces ambiguity. Section 8 still allows for 'Retained Information,' but the circumstances under which information can be retained are no longer specified, creating an in-text contradiction in Section 8 where this term is used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless otherwise requested in writing by the Disclosing Party within fifteen (15) days of the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement, or upon written notice given at any time during the Term of this Agreement requesting the return of the Disclosing P arty’s Confidential Information , following the expiration of the fifteen (15) day delay provided for above the Receiving Party shall destroy all tangible forms and destroy/delete all intangible forms of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information (includin g any originals and all copies thereof) in its possession. Notwithstanding the foregoing, t he Receiving Party shall be permitted to retain one copy of the Retained Information and such Retained Information shall be treated by the Receiving Party in accor dance with this Agreement. Upon written request by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall provide to the Disclosing Party or shall cause to be provided to the Disclosing Party, a written confirmation executed by a duly authorized officer of the Re ceiving Party that all originals and any copies of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information (other than Retained Information) or any portion thereof have been returned or destroyed , as the case may be, and shall no longer be used in any manner whatsoeve r by the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Retained Information' with specific conditions (legal requirement or electronic storage). The changed text removes these conditions, making the definition of 'Retained Information' broader and undefined. This contradicts the contradicted text (Section 8), which allows for retaining 'Retained Information' but relies on a specific understanding of what that information is. Without the original qualifying conditions, the Receiving Party could argue that any copy is 'Retained Information', nullifying the obligation to destroy all other copies. This creates a clear and direct conflict, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable regarding the return or destruction of confidential information.

perturbation.explanation: Removing the placeholder for the 'Effective Date' in Section 1 makes it undefined. The agreement relies on this date to determine the term and termination conditions, which contradicts Section 8 where 'Effective Date' is mentioned, making it practically impossible to enforce the agreement's duration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date, and unless earlier terminated in accordance herewith, shall continue in force for a period of [NUMBER OF] year(s) from the Effective Date. Notwithstanding the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement, the obligations of confidentiality with respect to any Confidential Information exchanged between the Parties prior to the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement, shall remain in full force and effect until such Confidential Information falls within the scope of the exceptions set out in Article 3.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text omits the definition of 'Effective Date', as explained in the Perturbation Explanation, rendering the 'Effective Date' undefined. The Contradicted Text states 'This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date...', which is now impossible to determine, directly conflicting with the Contradicted Text as it makes the agreement's commencement and duration unenforceable. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 8.2 A Party may terminate this Agreement, without cause, upon twenty (20) Business Days’ prior written notice to the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.2 A Party may terminate this Agreement upon prior written notice to the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the “without cause, upon twenty (20) Business Days’ prior written notice” portion of Section 8.2 makes it so that a Party can only terminate this agreement if they have a cause. This is an omission that causes an in-text contradiction within this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A Party may terminate this Agreement upon prior written notice to the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows termination 'without cause, upon twenty (20) Business Days’ prior written notice'. The changed text removes 'without cause, upon twenty (20) Business Days’ prior written notice', implying termination requires a cause. The explanation confirms this by stating the change now means a Party can only terminate if they have cause. This directly contradicts the contradicted text: 'A Party may terminate this Agreement upon prior written notice to the other Party', since that original text provided termination with no cause. The removal of 'without cause' creates a scenario where the conditions for termination have been altered, thus causing a direct conflict and an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: I further agree to ensure that all Concordia participants who will be provided access to Company’s Confidential Information, as such term is defined in the NDA, are informed of their obligations under such terms and conditions, and to procure from them an executed Intervention of a Participant, attached hereto as Appendix A, prior to providing them access to the Confidential Inform ation.
perturbation.changed_text: I further agree to ensure that all Concordia participants who will be provided access to Company’s Confidential Information, as such term is defined in the NDA, are informed of their obligations under such terms and conditions.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'and to procure from them an executed Intervention of a Participant, attached hereto as Appendix A, prior to providing them access to the Confidential Information', it creates an in-text contradiction due to a participant not having to fill out Appendix A. This makes it so that this 'Acknowledgement to a Non-Disclosure Agreement' has no effect on the following page of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INTERVENTION OF A PARTICIPANT IMPORTANT: IT IS THE RECIPIENT SCIENTIST’S RESPONSIBILITY TO ENSURE THAT THIS INTERVENTION IS SIGNED BY ALL PARTICIPANTS WHO SHALL BE RECEIVING CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION UNDER THE NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT AND COPIES ARE PROVIDED TO OOR. Whereas Concordia University (“ Concordia ”) has entered into a non-disclosure agreement dated _________ (the “NDA ”) with XXX (the “ Company ”); Whereas, I ______________________ understand that information related to discussions with the Company to explore a collaboration on XXXX , received, or accessed directly or indirectly, by me (the “ Confidential Information ”), is subject to the terms and conditions of the NDA; I hereby agree to: i. treat the Confidential Information as confidential at all times; ii. use the Confidential Information only as directed by Dr. _________________ ; iii. maintain the Confidential Information in a secure manner that restricts access by any individual not approved and/or directed by Dr. _______________________ ; iv. maintain the confidentiality of Confidential Information and not disclose Co nfidential Information to any third party; v. to return to Dr. ________________________ the Confidential Information, and all copies thereof, upon receipt of a written request to this effect from Dr. _____________________ and/or prior to the termination of m y studies/employment at Concordia; vi. cooperate with Concordia in ensuring compliance with all applicable export regulations and restrictions which may apply to the Confidential Information. Signature: Date:______
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates obtaining a signed 'Intervention of a Participant' (Appendix A) before providing access to confidential information. The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text explicitly states the 'RECIPIENT SCIENTIST’S RESPONSIBILITY TO ENSURE THAT THIS INTERVENTION IS SIGNED BY ALL PARTICIPANTS'. By removing the requirement to obtain the signed intervention, the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text's requirement for the scientist to ensure it is signed, creating a strong in-text contradiction because the intervention is not required anymore according to the changed text, but it is still required by the contradicted text.
